title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
JavaScript offers several levels of object immutability:

• const — only prevents reassignment of the variable binding; the object itself is still mutable.
• Object.freeze(obj) — shallow freeze: prevents adding, removing, or changing properties. Nested objects remain mutable.
• Object.seal(obj) — allows changing existing properties but prevents adding or removing.
• structuredClone(obj) — creates a true deep clone (ES2022, replaces JSON trick).

For true deep immutability, use recursive freezing or a library like Immer.
